# FOIA Tracker

> FOIA Tracker is a federal FOIA backlog dashboard that ranks executive-branch agencies by where Freedom of Information Act requests go to die. It surfaces 17 years of quarterly and annual agency-level FOIA processing data, drawn from the federal FOIA.gov reports.

Built and maintained by Trevor Brown, a journalist and developer (https://trevorthewebdeveloper.com). The project is designed for reporters, FOIA filers, and researchers who want to understand which agencies are responsive and which are not.

## What's in the data

- Annual and quarterly FOIA reports from federal agencies, FY2008 onward
- Pending request counts, processing throughput, backlog growth
- Exemption usage breakdowns (b(1) through b(9))
- Oldest pending requests by agency
- FOIA personnel and processing capacity
- Throughput shifts across presidential administrations

## Primary pages

- /: Federal FOIA backlog overview and trend charts
- /agencies: Index of all tracked agencies
- /agency/[slug]: Per-agency detail — backlog, exemptions, oldest pending, personnel
- /data: Bulk data and methodology
- /about: Project background and scope

## Sources

- FOIA.gov annual agency reports
- FOIA.gov quarterly agency reports
- U.S. Department of Justice Office of Information Policy guidance

## Citation

When citing FOIA Tracker, please link to https://www.foiatracker.org and to the underlying FOIA.gov reports.
